name: Parallel Heels
setup: Sitting upright, legs parallel, heels placed on pedal, hip width apart, fingers placed toward back of Chair, arms bent, elbows facing back

movement.action: Straighten legs (as far as possible)
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Bend legs approximately 90 degrees in knee joint
Push down through heels
Keep feet dorsiflexed and stable as if standing on floor
Maintain trunk stabilization

name: Parallel Toes
setup: Sitting upright, legs parallel, toes placed on pedal, hip width apart, fingers placed toward back of Chair, arms bent, elbows facing back

movement.action: Straighten legs (as far as possible)
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Bend legs approximately 90 degrees in knee joint
Push pedal down through toes
Keep heels stable throughout moving around ankle joint
Maintain trunk stabilization

name: V-Position Toes
setup: Sitting upright, legs externally rotated, toes placed on pedal, heels together in small V position, fingers placed toward back of Chair, arms bent, elbows facing back

movement.action: Straighten legs (as far as possible)
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Bend legs approximately 90 degrees in knee joint
Keep knees aligned over toes
Keep heels squeezing together and stable
Maintain hip adductor engagement
Maintain trunk stabilization

name: Open V-Position Heels
setup: Sitting upright, legs externally rotated, heels placed on pedal in wide V position, fingers placed toward back of Chair, arms bent, elbows facing back

movement.action: Straighten legs (as far as possible)
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Bend legs approximately 90 degrees in knee joint
Push down through heels
Keep feet dorsiflexed and stable as if standing on the floor
Maintain hip adductor engagement
Maintain trunk stabilization

name: Open V-Position Toes
setup: Sitting upright, legs externally rotated, toes placed on pedal in wide V position, feet plantarflexed, fingers placed toward back of Chair, arms bent, elbows facing back

movement.action: Straighten legs (as far as possible)
Bend legs, return to starting position

cues: Keep knees aligned over toes
Push down through toes
Maintain adductor engagement
Keep heels stable throughout moving around ankle joint
Maintain trunk stabilization

name: Calf Raises
setup: Standing in front of Wunda Chair, toes of one foot placed on pedal, knee (just below) anchored on front of Chair, back leg straight, heel reaching toward floor, hands on sides of Chair, body on diagonal line

movement.action: Plantarflex foot, pressing pedal down
Dorsiflex foot, return to start position

cues: Maintain neutral pelvis
Maximize range of motion of ankle joint
Keep legs parallel with back heel pressing toward floor

name: Single Leg Heel
setup: Sitting upright, legs parallel, one heel placed on pedal, other leg straight and horizontal to floor, foot plantarflexed, fingers placed toward back of Chair, arms bent, elbows facing back

movement.action: Straighten leg (as far as possible)
Bend leg, return to start position

cues: Maintain equal weight on sit bones
Avoid trunk rotation
Keep working foot dorsiflexed and stable as if standing on floor
Keep supporting leg stable

name: Single Leg Toes
setup: Sitting upright, legs parallel, toes placed on pedal, other leg straight and horizontal to floor, foot plantarflexed, fingers placed toward back of Chair, arms bent, elbows facing back

movement.action: Straighten leg (as far as possible)
Bend leg, return to start position

cues: Push down through toes
Maintain equal weight on sit bones
Avoid trunk rotation
Keep supporting leg stable

name: Pike Standing
setup: Standing in front of Wunda Chair, legs straight and parallel, roll down placing hands on pedal, shoulders over wrists, lower pedal to base of Chair

movement.action: Lift trunk, shoulders in line with hip joints
Lower trunk
Lift trunk, shoulders in line with hip joints
Lower trunk
Roll up to standing position, return to start position

cues: Maximize spinal flexion
Maintain scapular stabilization
Maintain perpendicular alignment of legs
Keep head aligned with spine, eyes directed toward legs

name: Pike Standing Reverse
setup: Standing in back of Wunda Chair, legs straight and parallel, roll down placing hands on pedal in line with shoulders, flatten trunk into neutral spine position

movement.action: Round trunk
Flatten trunk into neutral spine position
Round trunk
Flatten trunk into neutral spine position
Roll up to standing position, return to start position

cues: Maximize spinal flexion when lifting pedal, shoulders are slightly above hips
Maintain perpendicular alignment of legs, equal weight on feet
Keep head aligned with spine

name: Leg Press Standing
setup: Standing in front of Wunda Chair, one foot placed on pedal, plantarflexed, leg straight, other leg and trunk perpendicular to floor, arms in T position, palms facing down

movement.action: Bend leg and straighten down
Bend leg and straighten forward, return to start position

cues: Maintain perpendicular alignment of standing leg and trunk
Initiate movement with hamstrings
Maintain plantarflexion of foot on pedal
Straighten leg fully at bottom and top of movement

name: Side Over
setup: Sitting sideways on Wunda Chair, one leg straight, foot plantarflexed, touching floor, other leg bent in front of Chair, heel hooked around back of Chair, one hand placed on pedal, shoulder over wrist, other hand reaching overhead, palm facing ceiling, shoulder internally rotated, body on diagonal line

movement.action: Arc trunk sideways, lowering pedal
Straighten and lift trunk, raising pedal, return to start position

objectives: Spinal lateral flexor control and stretch
cues: Maintain co-contraction of abdominals and back extensors
Keep straight leg in contact with floor
Begin and end movement with trunk and leg on diagonal line
Imagine moving between two panes of glass

name: Swan
setup: Lying prone on Wunda Chair, hands placed on pedal, shoulders over wrists (as close as possible), legs straight and together, body parallel to floor

movement.action: Raise trunk, lifting pedal
Lower trunk, pressing pedal down, return to start position

cues: Maximize spinal articulation in both directions
Keep legs stable
Maintain abdominal engagement
Press hands into pedal when lifting using shoulder extensors

name: Pike Sitting
setup: Sitting on mat facing Wunda Chair, legs straight and together, feet plantarflexed on front edge of chair, arms straight, shoulder width apart, hands placed on pedal, trunk in C curve position

movement.action: Lower arms, pressing pedal down
Raise arms, lifting pedal up, return to start position

cues: Keep arms straight
Keep legs straight and together
Maintain scapular stabilization
Maintain C curve position

name: Pike Forward
setup: Standing on pedal facing Wunda Chair, legs straight, hip width apart, feet plantarflexed, arms straight, shoulders over wrists, hands placed on outside of back edge of chair, trunk rounded

movement.action: Raise body, lifting pedal up
Lower body, pressing pedal down (without touching bottom)
Lower body, return to start position

cues: Maximize spinal flexion
Keep shoulders over wrists, avoid shoulders moving forward
Maintain scapular stabilization
Keep head aligned with spine, eyes directed toward body

name: Pelvic Curl
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs bent, hip apart, feet placed on pedal in Prehensile position (page 24 Foundation Reformer MAWB), pedal pressed down, arms by sides, palms down

movement.action: Roll pelvis and spine up
Pause
Roll spine and pelvis down, return to start position
Pause

cues: Maintain prehensile position
Maximize lumbar flexion as pelvis and spine roll up and down
Articulate through each vertebra as spine rolls up and down
Return to neutral spine position after each repetition

name: Jackknife
setup: Lying supine in front of Wunda Chair in Roll Over position (page 42 Mat MAWB), arms straight, shoulder width apart, hands holding pedal, palms facing up

movement.action: Lift legs, perpendicular to mat
Roll over
Lower legs, lift legs toward ceiling, body reaching vertical line (as close as possible)
Roll down, feet opposite face, return to start position

cues: Utilize hip, back and shoulder extensors to achieve vertical line

name: Cat Stretch Kneeling
setup: Kneeling upright on Wunda Chair, close to front edge, facing forward, legs parallel, hip width apart, roll down, trunk rounded, arms straight, hands placed on pedal, palms facing down, fingers facing forward, shoulder and hip joints aligned horizontally

movement.action: Roll down, flattening trunk and lowering pedal
Roll up, rounding trunk and lifting pedal
Continue to roll up, return to start position

cues: Maintain hips aligned over knees (as close as possible)
Lift pedal until shoulders are slightly above hips
Reach crown of head towards pedal when rolling down
Place head between arms when trunk is flat and pedal down

name: Shrugs
setup: Sitting upright (on box) with back to Wunda Chair, legs bent and parallel, hip width apart, arms straight, shoulder width apart, hands placed on pedal, palms facing back

movement.action: Raise shoulders, lifting pedal up
Lower shoulders, pressing pedal down, return to start position

cues: Maintain trunk stabilization
Keep body upright
Keep arms straight
Elevate shoulders directly upward toward ears

name: Triceps Press Sit
setup: Starting in Shrugs position (page 23)

movement.action: Bend arms, lifting pedal
Straighten arms, lowering pedal, return to start position

cues: Maintain trunk stabilization
Maintain upright position
Maintain scapular stabilization
Keep elbows facing back, parallel to each other

name: Triceps Prone
setup: Lying prone on Wunda Chair, arms straight, shoulders over wrists, hands placed on pedal, legs straight and together, feet plantarflexed, body parallel to mat

movement.action: Bend arms, lifting pedal
Straighten arms, lowering pedal, return to start position

cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Keep legs and trunk stable and parallel to mat
Keep elbows close to sides of trunk when bent

name: Frog Back
setup: Facing back to Wunda Chair, arms bent, elbows facing back, hands placed on front edge of chair, palms facing back, legs bent, hips externally rotated, feet plantarflexed, toes on pedal, heels together, small V position

movement.action: Straighten arms, lifting pedal
Bend arms, lowering pedal, return to start position

cues: Keep elbows facing back, parallel to each other
Keep distance between heels and pelvis consistent (small)

name: Side Arm Kneeling
setup: Kneeling side in front of Wunda Chair, legs parallel, hip width apart, trunk in lateral flexion, head rotated, eyes focused down, inside arm straight, hand placed on pedal, palm facing down, fingers forward, outside arm overhead, internally rotated, elbow slightly bent, palm facing up

movement.action: Bend arm, lifting pedal
Straighten arm, lowering pedal, return to start position

cues: Keep long line of outside arm and wrist consistent
Direct elbow back when bending and straightening arm

name: Hamstring Curl
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, heels placed on pedal, feet dorsiflexed, legs straight, hip width apart, arms by sides, palms down

movement.action: Bend legs, lowering pedal
Straighten legs, lifting pedal, return to start position

cues: Maintain neutral pelvis
Initiate movement using hamstrings
Focus on knee flexion
Take pedal approximately halfway down

name: Hip Opener
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs bent, hips externally rotated, sides of feet placed on pedal, soles of feet pressed together, pedal halfway down, arms by sides, palms down

movement.action: Lower legs, pressing pedal down
Raise legs, lifting pedal, return to start position

cues: Maintain neutral pelvis
Maximize work of hip external rotators
Reach knees out and down toward mat
Focus on hip extension, minimizing movement in knee joints

name: Lunge Forward Prep
setup: Standing upright facing Wunda Chair, hands placed on poles, poles in line with sternum, one foot on Wunda Chair, leg bent, knee aligned over ankle, other foot placed on pedal, plantarflexed, leg straight

movement.action: Straighten leg, lifting pedal
Bend leg, lowering pedal, return to start position (without touching bottom)

cues: Maintain upright alignment
Maintain pelvic and trunk stabilization
Initiate movement with hip extensors
Keep hip joint aligned over pedal (as close as possible) and knee behind or aligned with toes when lifting and lowering pedal

name: Step Down Back
setup: Standing upright facing Wunda Chair, one foot on Wunda Chair, leg bent, knee aligned over ankle, thigh parallel to mat, other foot placed on pedal, plantarflexed, leg bent, arms crossed in front of body, parallel to floor, trunk flat on diagonal line, pedal hovering

movement.action: Straighten back leg, lowering pedal
Bend back leg, lifting pedal, return to start position

cues: Maintain pelvic stabilization
Maintain plantarflexion of back foot
Keep knee aligned over ankle (as close as possible)

name: Frog Front
setup: Standing on pedal facing Wunda Chair, legs bent, hips externally rotated, feet plantarflexed, heels together to form V position, arms straight, hands on outside of front edge of chair, shoulders over wrists, fingers facing outward, trunk flat on diagonal, pedal lifted

movement.action: Straighten legs, lowering pedal (without touching bottom)
Bend legs, lifting pedal, return to start position

cues: Keep heels pressing together
Utilize pumping-action as legs straighten and bend
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Torso Press Sit
setup: Sitting facing back of Wunda Chair, arms straight, shoulder width apart, hands placed on pedal, fingers facing away from body, legs straight and together, parallel to mat, feet plantarflexed

movement.action: Lower trunk, pressing pedal down
Raise trunk, lifting pedal up, return to start position

cues: Maintain trunk stabilization
Keep head aligned with spine
Keep legs stable
Hinge around hip and shoulder joints

name: Tendon Stretch
setup: Facing back to Wunda Chair, trunk rounded, arms straight, shoulder width apart, hands placed on front edge of chair, palms down, fingers wrapped around, legs straight, hip width apart, feet plantarflexed

movement.action: Raise body, lifting pedal up
Lower body, pressing pedal down (without touching bottom)
Lower body, return to start position

cues: Lift pelvis upward, maximizing spinal flexion
Keep head aligned with spine, eyes directed toward body
Maintain scapular stabilization

name: Side Stretch Kneeling
setup: Kneeling side in front of Wunda Chair, legs parallel, hip width apart, inside arm straight, hand placed on pedal, palm facing down, fingers forward, outside arm overhead, internally rotated, elbow slightly bent, palm facing up

movement.action: Arc trunk sideways, lowering pedal
Straighten and lift trunk, raising pedal, return to start position

cues: Keep pelvis and lower body stable
Maintain scapular stabilization
Maintain co-contraction of abdominals and back extensors
Imagine moving between two panes of glass

name: Pike Side
setup: Standing side to Wunda Chair, trunk rounded, legs straight, feet placed on pedal, inside leg in front, arms straight, shoulders above wrists, hands placed on side edge of chair, fingers wrapped around front and back of chair

movement.action: Raise body, lifting pedal
Lower body, pressing pedal down (without touching bottom), return to start position

cues: Lift pelvis upward
Keep shoulders over wrists
Keep head aligned with spine, eyes directed toward body

name: Swan on Floor
setup: Lying prone on mat facing Wunda Chair, arms straight, shoulder width apart, hands placed on pedal, legs straight and together, feet plantarflexed

movement.action: Lift trunk, simultaneously lower pedal
Lower trunk, simultaneously lift pedal, return to start position

cues: Maintain trunk stabilization
Maintain scapular stabilization
Keep trunk stable as pedal lightly touches base
Maximize spinal articulation in both directions

name: Swan Single Arm
setup: Lying prone on Wunda Chair, arms straight, one hand placed on pedal, shoulder over wrist (as close as possible), other arm out to side, palm facing down, legs straight and together, body parallel to floor

movement.action: Raise trunk, lifting pedal
Lower trunk, pressing pedal down, return to start position

cues: Maximize spinal articulation in both directions
Keep legs and outstretched arm stable
Press hands into pedal when lifting using shoulder extensors
